content.problemStatement:
DAIICT college students want to attend an IPL match.

A total of NNN students from the college want to go while only MMM tickets are available for the match.

Determine how many students won't be able to book tickets.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of students wants to go and the total number of tickets available, respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of students who won't be able to book tickets.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and M — the number of students who want to attend and the number of tickets available.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line: the number of students who will not be able to book a ticket.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 5 students, 3 tickets → 5‑3 = 2 cannot get tickets.
Test case 2: 5 students, 7 tickets → all get tickets, answer 0.
Test case 3: 4 students, 1 ticket → 4‑1 = 3 cannot get tickets.
Test case 4: 8 students, 8 tickets → answer 0.
First test: 10‑3 = 7 students left without tickets. Second test: 2 ≤ 5, so everyone gets a ticket → 0.

hidden.label: edge case where tickets exceed students

solution.approach: For each test case we compare N and M. If N > M, the excess N‑M students cannot get tickets; otherwise every student can get a ticket, so the answer is 0. This is a direct O(1) computation per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time operation for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[idx]); m = int(data[idx + 1]); idx += 2
        out.append(str(n - m if n > m else 0))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
